pages.title: API standards
pages.content:
# API standards — Medbot

Monorepo **pnpm** + **Turborepo**: backend **Express 5** (JavaScript), frontend **Next.js 16** (TypeScript).

## 1. Base path
* All HTTP routes live under the **/api** prefix (e.g. **/api/auth/login**, **/api/chat/stream**).
* **No** `/v1`-style version segment in URLs today.
* Production: the web app usually rewrites **/api/*** to the backend origin via **NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL** (no trailing slash).

## 2. Format
* Request/response: **JSON**, UTF-8.
* Body fields use **camelCase** (e.g. **question**, **sessionId**, **isPinned**).
* Streaming chat: **POST /api/chat/stream** returns **SSE** (`text/event-stream`), not WebSocket.

## 3. HTTP methods (as implemented)
* **GET**: health, list sessions, history, search logs.
* **POST**: login, chat (JSON or SSE), session cleanup.
* **PATCH**: session metadata (title, pinned).
* **DELETE**: delete session.

## 4. Pagination & query (chat sessions)
* **GET /api/chat/sessions**: query **limit**, **offset**, **q** (filter title), **pinnedOnly=true**.
* **GET /api/chat/search**: **q** (minimum 2 characters), **limit**, **offset**.

## 5. Rate limit
* In-memory middleware per IP; headers **X-RateLimit-Limit**, **X-RateLimit-Remaining**.
* **429**: body often includes **retryAfterMs** (limit window).

pages.title: Authentication Flow
pages.content:
# Authentication — JWT

## 1. Mechanism
* **One JWT** after login (no refresh token in this codebase yet).
* Payload: **sub** (user id), **email**; signed with **JWT_SECRET**; lifetime **JWT_EXPIRES_IN** (env).
* Passwords stored with **bcrypt** in **app_users** (Supabase Postgres), RLS enabled; API uses **service role** when it must read/write users.

## 2. Flow
1. **POST /api/auth/login** — body **{ email, password }** → **{ token, user }**.
2. Client sends **Authorization: Bearer** with the JWT for protected routes.
3. **GET /api/auth/me** — Bearer → **{ user: { id, email } }**.
4. Chat: all **/api/chat/*** except **GET /api/chat/health** require Bearer.

## 3. Security
* Production: HTTPS.
* Do not commit secrets; only env templates belong in git.

pages.title: RESTful Endpoints Reference
pages.content:
# Endpoint reference (matches this repo)

Base: API origin (e.g. dev **http://localhost:5000** — from **PORT** env).

## Auth — /api/auth
* **POST /api/auth/login** — no auth. Body: email, password.
* **GET /api/auth/me** — Bearer.

## Chat — /api/chat
* **GET /api/chat/health** — no auth.
* **POST /api/chat/message** — Bearer. Body: **question**, **sessionId?** → JSON answer.
* **POST /api/chat/stream** — Bearer. Same body as above → **SSE** (stream_start, stream_chunk, stream_end / stream_error).
* **GET /api/chat/sessions** — Bearer. Query: limit, offset, q, pinnedOnly.
* **GET /api/chat/session/:sessionId/history** — Bearer.
* **GET /api/chat/search** — Bearer. Query: q, limit, offset.
* **PATCH /api/chat/session/:sessionId** — Bearer. Body: title?, isPinned?
* **DELETE /api/chat/session/:sessionId** — Bearer.
* **POST /api/chat/session/:sessionId/cleanup** — Bearer.

## Notes
* RAG and vector search have **no** separate REST surface — they run inside message/stream handling.
* Vector knowledge lives in **Zilliz Cloud**, not an upload endpoint in this API.

pages.title: RAG & Vector Search (Medbot)
pages.content:
# RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)

## 1. Concept
**RAG** augments the LLM answer with knowledge chunks from **Zilliz Cloud** (Milvus): embed question → cosine similarity → top-K → merge into the prompt.

## 2. Chat flow (backend)
0. **Simple greetings** (hi/hello/hey) → fixed reply, no full LLM call.
1. **Normalizer**: medical abbreviations (static map + UMLS lookup via scalar filter on Zilliz).
2. **Embed query**: **@xenova/transformers** Nomic ONNX, **768 dimensions**, prefixes per embedding contract (default **search_query:** + space).
3. **Search**: Zilliz, **COSINE** metric, **AUTOINDEX**, filter **is_active == true**, score ≥ MIN_RELEVANCE.
4. **Rerank**: dedupe, boost UMLS/CUI/Q&A, keep **MATCH_COUNT** (5).
5. **confidence** = average similarity: if **below HIGH_RELEVANCE** (0.65) → OneMin **without** KB, on error Gemini; **from 0.65 up** → OneMin **with** KB.

## 3. Ingest
* Parquet UMLS (licensed) → **Python** scripts (torch, transformers, **pymilvus**).
* Document prefix must match contract; upsert: ingest_key, embedding, content, source_type, metadata_json, umls_str_lower, is_active.

## 4. Config (constants)
* MIN_RELEVANCE_SCORE **0.35**, HIGH_RELEVANCE_SCORE **0.65**, MATCH_COUNT **5**.
* Changing model/prefix/dim → **re-embed** the whole collection.

## 5. Postgres
* Supabase stores **chat_sessions**, **chat_logs**, **app_users**, **ingestion_file_state** — **not** RAG vectors.

pages.title: Error Handling & Status Codes
pages.content:
# Errors & HTTP codes

## 1. Central error middleware
* JSON response: **{ error: string }**.
* If **NODE_ENV !== production**, **detail** may include the original error message for debugging.

## 2. Common codes
* **200** — OK.
* **400** — body/query validation failed.
* **401** — missing/invalid Bearer JWT (or invalid email/password on login).
* **429** — rate limit: **error**, **message**, **retryAfterMs**.
* **500** — unhandled error or **JWT_SECRET** not configured (auth middleware).

## 3. SSE
* **stream_error** event with payload **{ message }** when the stream pipeline fails.

pages.title: Git Branching Strategy
pages.content:
# Git — suggestions

* **main**: stable branch, production deploys.
* **develop**: integration (if the team uses it).
* **feature/**, **bugfix/**, **hotfix/** + descriptive names — per team convention.
* Merge/PR rules (approvals, required CI) are for the team to decide.

pages.title: Code Review Process
pages.content:
# Code review

* RAG: any **constants** changes (thresholds, MATCH_COUNT) need rationale + test no-KB / edge scores.
* Embedding contract changes → plan **re-embed Zilliz**.
* No PII in logs; no hardcoded secrets.
* **pnpm lint** (Turbo) before merge; **Husky** + **lint-staged** may block console.log/debugger in apps/api and apps/web.

pages.title: CI/CD Pipeline
pages.content:
# CI/CD — as in this repo

## Local / PR
* **pnpm lint** — Turbo runs lint in workspaces that define it (web: ESLint).
* **pnpm build** — Next build and dependent tasks.
* The **api** package has no meaningful **test** script yet (placeholder); add tests as needed.

## Deploy
* No required GitHub Actions pipeline is described in the root manifest — configure CI per org (GitHub/GitLab/Vercel).

pages.title: Testing Strategy
pages.content:
# Testing

* **Frontend**: ESLint (**eslint-config-next**).
* **Backend JS**: manual checks + **GET /api/chat/health**; Jest/Vitest can be added later.
* **Python scripts** (UMLS export/ingest): integration against Parquet + Zilliz per environment.
* E2E (Playwright/Cypress): optional, not required in-repo today.

pages.title: Coding Standards & Conventions
pages.content:
# Coding standards

## Backend (apps/api)
* **JavaScript** CommonJS, **Express** routes → controllers → services → providers.
* camelCase for variables/functions; app constants in **config/constants**.

## Frontend (apps/web)
* **TypeScript**, React 19, App Router; Next ESLint.

## General
* **DRY**, **KISS**; identifiers should be clear English names.

pages.title: Environment Setup Guide
pages.content:
# Development environment

## Requirements
* **Node.js 18+**, **pnpm 10** (root `packageManager`).
* **Python 3.10+** if you run UMLS export/ingest.
* Accounts: **Supabase** (Postgres), **Zilliz Cloud**, **1min.ai** and **Gemini** API keys.

## Steps
1. **pnpm install** (root).
2. **pnpm setup:env** — copy env templates if missing.
3. Fill **DATABASE_URL**, **SUPABASE_***, **JWT_SECRET**, **ZILLIZ_***, provider keys in backend env.
4. **pnpm bootstrap** — Postgres schema + seed user (if **SEED_*** set).
5. In api workspace: **pnpm zilliz:setup** — create vector collection.
6. (Recommended) **pnpm nomic:cache** — download ONNX model.
7. **pip install -r** the Python manifest under api `scripts/` (pyarrow, torch, transformers, pymilvus, …).
8. **pnpm dev** — Turbo: web (often **3000**) + api (default **PORT** **5000**).

## Not part of the default flow
* This repo does **not** require Docker Compose / Redis / Alembic for the chat app.

pages.title: Deployment Runbook
pages.content:
# Deployment — checklist

## Web (e.g. Vercel)
* Project root **apps/web**; **NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL** points to production API (no trailing slash).

## API
* Host Node separately (VM, container, PaaS) with full env; default **cors()** — tighten origin in production.

## Data
* Back up **Supabase** per your plan; **Zilliz** cluster per provider console.

## Rollback
* Deploy previous image/commit; verify health and Zilliz.

pages.title: Monitoring & Alerting
pages.content:
# Monitoring

## Application
* JSON logs to stdout: **LOG_LEVEL** (error, warn, info, http, debug).
* **requestId** on requests for tracing.
* **GET /api/chat/health** — smoke / load balancer.

## Outside this repo
* Metrics/logs (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Sentry) are infrastructure-level — not pre-wired in app code.

pages.title: Security Best Practices
pages.content:
# Security — medical-chatbot

* **JWT**: strong **JWT_SECRET**; HTTPS in production.
* **Passwords**: bcrypt (**bcryptjs**); never return hashes to the client.
* **Supabase RLS** on **app_users** — server uses service role when needed.
* **Input**: schema validation middleware for chat/auth bodies; **question** length limits.
* **Zilliz**: token server-only; never expose to the browser.

pages.title: Secret Management
pages.content:
# Secrets

* **Do not** commit real env files.
* Dev: local **apps/api** and **apps/web** env from templates in the repo.
* Prod: secret manager / platform env vars (Vercel, Fly, K8s secrets, …).
* **ONEMIN**, **GEMINI**, **ZILLIZ**, **SUPABASE** keys are server-side only (except public Supabase URL if needed).

pages.title: OWASP Compliance Checklist
pages.content:
# OWASP — repo perspective

1. **Access control**: JWT for chat; controllers check user ownership on sessions.
2. **Crypto**: bcrypt passwords; TLS in production.
3. **Injection**: Supabase/PostgREST and Zilliz SDKs — avoid manual SQL string concatenation in JS.
4. **Secrets**: no keys hardcoded in source.
5. **Logging**: do not log bodies with secrets; request logger redacts sensitive metadata.
